list.name: Changes
list.comment: Remove expiration logic using ".clockSkewSeconds(Integer.MAX_VALUE) " because all Gateway will be the only place for expiration check

task.summary: Default task
update jwt.expireation for access-token/refresh-token

Create AuthFilter in geospatial-service
1 add dependencies for JWT token 2 create JwtUtil 3 create CustomUserDetailService 4 add jwt.expiration properties in geospatial-service.yml 5 create JwtAuthFilter 6 add JwtAuthFilter to GeospatialServiceConfig
Nothing but format the indentation
spring-boot-starter-web dependency was missing so service was not able to start. no idea !!!!
miss commit for JwtAuthFilter creation
gateway-service 1. token null check has been added JwtTokenNotExistException and GlobalExceptionHandler has been updated 2. remove jwtUtil validation and use just getClaims instead
Jwt.expiration doesn't required except security-service
Add JwtAuthFilter in security-service and add logic to check the null token also add null token check in the geospatial for just in case

First try of MapStruct framework using instead BeanUtil class 1. create XXXMapper class 2. add mapstruct dependency 3. finally remove bean util and replace with Mapper
so what is benefit of using a mapstruct instead bean-util ????? 1. Compile-time Type Safety MapStruct generates the mapping code at compile time, which means that any issues with the mapping (such as missing fields or incompatible types) are caught during compilation
2. MapStruct generates bytecode directly, making the mapping process much faster than reflection-based solutions like BeanUtils.copyProperties.
3.Maintainability and Readability MapStruct allows for a more declarative style of defining mappings. The mappings are clear and concise, and they are directly within the interface, which improves code readability and maintainability. This contrasts with BeanUtils.copyProperties, which can result in boilerplate code and less clarity about what fields are being mapped.
4. Custom Mappings MapStruct provides more flexibility and control over the mapping process. You can define custom mappings for specific fields, use expressions, and apply conversions during the mapping. This level of customization is more complex to achieve with BeanUtils.copyProperties.
5. Less Boilerplate Code Using MapStruct reduces the amount of boilerplate code. With BeanUtils.copyProperties, you need to write additional code to handle special cases, whereas MapStruct can handle these within the mapping interface, keeping your service layer cleaner.

MapStruct has been move to Parent pom.xml from security-service method name has been renamed with first small letter Mapper doesn't need spring been injection any more just access INSTANCE directly from interface SINCE @Mapper annotation will do the job for me
Class name refactoring for AuthDTO.java and AuthRequest.java MemberDTO and MemberRegisterRequest and MemberRegisterResponse
All BeanUtil has been removed and replaced with MapStruct !!!!!!
install dev-tool for all service except for Eureka
Add /auth/refreshToken access point for everyone => it doesn't mean that this point won't go through authentication process . every single request will still going authentication process.
Add /auth/refreshToken access point for everyone => it doesn't mean that this point won't go through authentication process . every single request will still going authentication process.

Refresh Token function has been added
1. Refresh token process going through JwtAuthFiler and not skipping Token validation which is checking user's data from local server throughout CustomUserDetailService (and also basic validation on the Gateway)
and create both access and refresh token .
Since i dont want any other input value but just refresh token in here using HttpServletRequest is enough and clean.
Refactoring AuthController to remove all business logic to service layer and change mapping name from getToken to login

Refactoring name of authenticateManager to customAuthenticationManager and add comment for different layer customization from SecurityServiceConfig whcih is either AuthenticationMAnager level or AuthentiocationProvider level
Name of module security-service change to auth-service

common-service now have a spring-boot-starter-data-jpa and spring-boot-starter-security
Gateway-service Geospatial-service Web-service doesn't need data-jpa so exclude from common-lib

Create Token Store Function when user register
1. Token, TokenDTO , TokenMapper , TokenRepository TokenMApper.tokenTDOToToken has a very special function that transform MemberDTO property to Member !!!!!
2. AuthService include token store logic using member-service 3. Member has typo for channelId 4. MemberController / MemberService add token store method 5. MemberServiceApplication now able to create the bean that has been imported from common-service

Create Token Store Function when user register
1. Token, TokenDTO , TokenMapper , TokenRepository TokenMApper.tokenTDOToToken has a very special function that transform MemberDTO property to Member !!!!!
2. AuthService include token store logic using member-service 3. Member has typo for channelId 4. MemberController / MemberService add token store method 5. MemberServiceApplication now able to create the bean that has been imported from common-service
MemberMapper has been moved from under dto package to under com.adventuretube.auth.
Properties for maven compiler and sourceEncoding has been moved up to parents
Properties for maven compiler and sourceEncoding has been moved up to parents
add kafka-service using a docker

add logout function AuthController AuthService method name authenticate change to loginWithIdAndPassword add logout method add "/auth/logout" to AuthServiceConfig
add required function in member-service MemberController findToken deleteAllToken MemberService findMemberByEmailAndTokenCheck findToken deleteAllToken
TokenRepository deleteAppTokenByAccessToken => to revoke all existing token before create the new one
Test Basic kafka to geospatial-service
Setting mDNS(muliticast DNS) for Eureka server and Kafka server in the rasberry-pi
kafka setting and test Kafka configuration has to be ip address
Token repository delete will look access or refreshToken together
Rename RestAPIErrorResponse.java to RestAPIResponse
Remove ResponseEntity from Service LAyer
Change Docker Compose file name for storages
just add developer link as a commend
Nothing but Eureka server change from strider-pi to local
Member and Token id field change to UUID and return value after register member will return that UUID to iOS application
Nothing but change to localhost
Auto indentation
update logging setting
Add logging
Add comment for the How JWT filter and Spring Security work together
Dockerfile not in use
Add CreateAt Token table
Add MongoDB and Json dependency and Model to Common-module
refactoring all mongo db to move from Common to geospatial-service
All GlobalExceptionHandler update for error message that include where the error come from
ios will not send user information

1 MemberRegisterResponse : remove userDetails object and add userId only for security reason
2 refactory register/login method to user google idToken only without using any other information for security reason

MESSAGE.value:
What was changed:
- Removed unreachable code: No more if/else logic - Simplified to success path only: Controller only handles successful deletion - Exception delegation: When user not found, MemberNotFoundException flows to GlobalExceptionHandler
Now the flow is:
1. Success case: User exists → deleted → HTTP 200 OK 2. Failure case: User not found → MemberNotFoundException → GlobalExceptionHandler → HTTP 404 NOT_FOUND
This matches your documentation's Approach 2: GlobalExceptionHandler Delegation for business operations that require follow-up processing.

⏺ Add deployment script for complete service rebuild
- Created adventuretube-service-redeploy-for-current-branch.sh - Automates Maven clean/package, Docker cleanup, and fresh deployment - Includes error handling and status reporting for reliable rebuilds

⏺ Fix deployment script environment and Docker Compose issues
- Add proper environment file parameter handling with validation - Update all commands from docker-compose to docker compose - Ensure consistent env file usage throughout script execution - Add usage instructions for proper script invocation
remove git pulling session
update docker-compose-kafka.yml

1. Removes old Docker images specifically for your services (step 6.5) 2. Uses --no-cache flag to force fresh builds (step 7) 3. Adds --volumes --remove-orphans for complete cleanup (step 6)
This ensures: - No cached Docker layers from previous builds - Fresh compilation of your Java code changes - Complete cleanup of old containers and volumes - Prevention of "stale cache" issues
The script now provides a true clean rebuild every time you run it.
remove "--remove-orphans"

1. Decodes the JWT token to extract the payload 2. Compares the aud (audience) claim with your expected client ID 3. Logs both values for debugging 4. Returns null early if there's a mismatch 5. Only proceeds with Google's verifier if the client ID matches
syntax error

1. Skip JWT validation for health check requests 2. Stop the spam logs you're seeing every 10 seconds 3. Allow Docker health checks to work without authentication 4. Improve performance by not processing JWT tokens for health checks
add web-client dependency
R2DBC configuration (replaces datasource/JPA)

Test Refactor: Migrate MemberController to functional RouterFunction + Handler
Replace annotation-based @RestController with Spring WebFlux functional endpoints (RouterFunction + HandlerFunction) for idiomatic reactive API design.
- Add MemberHandler with 7 handler methods (ServerRequest → Mono<ServerResponse>) - Add MemberRouterConfig with RouterFunction bean chaining all POST routes - Add GlobalWebExceptionHandler (WebExceptionHandler) replacing @ControllerAdvice which does not work with functional endpoints - Delete MemberController and GlobalExceptionHandler
URL paths and ServiceResponse JSON format remain unchanged — no caller-side (auth-service) changes needed.

Step 0+1: Migrate auth-service security layer from Servlet to WebFlux
── Step 0: ServiceClient reactive + dependency cleanup ──
common-api/ServiceClient.java (MODIFIED) - Add postReactive() and getReactive() methods returning Mono<ServiceResponse<T>> instead of calling .block(), enabling non-blocking reactive pipelines - Existing blocking post()/get() unchanged for other services
auth-service/pom.xml (MODIFIED) - Remove spring-boot-starter-web: eliminates servlet container (Tomcat), auth-service now runs on Netty via spring-boot-starter-webflux - Remove spring-boot-starter-data-jpa: auth-service has no database, all data operations go through REST calls to member-service - Remove postgresql driver: same reason, no direct DB access
── Step 1: Security Servlet → WebFlux ──
Security Config: AuthServiceConfig.java (MODIFIED) - @EnableWebSecurity → @EnableWebFluxSecurity: Spring Security's servlet config does not work on the reactive (Netty) stack - SecurityFilterChain → SecurityWebFilterChain: WebFlux equivalent - HttpSecurity → ServerHttpSecurity: reactive security DSL - New ReactiveAuthenticationManager bean replaces the servlet-based AuthenticationManager + CustomAuthenticationProvider chain
JWT Filter: JwtWebFilter.java (NEW) - Replaces JwtAuthFilter because OncePerRequestFilter is a servlet filter that depends on HttpServletRequest/HttpServletResponse, which don't exist in the reactive stack - Implements WebFilter (reactive equivalent) using ServerWebExchange - Sets authentication via ReactiveSecurityContextHolder (context-propagation) instead of thread-local SecurityContextHolder
JwtAuthFilter.java (DELETED) - Extends OncePerRequestFilter (servlet API), incompatible with Netty/WebFlux
User Details: CustomUserDetailService.java (MODIFIED) - UserDetailsService → ReactiveUserDetailsService: the servlet interface's blocking loadUserByUsername() would block the Netty event loop - findByUsername() returns Mono<UserDetails>, using postReactive() for non-blocking member-service calls
Authentication Provider: CustomAuthenticationProvider.java (DELETED) - Extends DaoAuthenticationProvider which internally calls the blocking UserDetailsService.loadUserByUsername(), incompatible with reactive stack - Same logic (find user → compare password → return auth token) now lives in ReactiveAuthenticationManager bean in AuthServiceConfig as a reactive lambda chain
Unused JPA Entities: Member.java (DELETED) - JPA @Entity requiring Hibernate/JPA runtime which was removed from pom.xml - Auth-service has no database; member data comes from member-service via REST Token.java (DELETED) - Same reason: JPA entity with @ManyToOne relationship, never used since token storage is handled by member-service
Adapted for compilation (will be fully rewritten in Step 2): AuthService.java (MODIFIED) - Remove @Transactional: no JPA/datasource exists in auth-service - AuthenticationManager → ReactiveAuthenticationManager with temporary .block() call until Step 2 converts to full reactive chain - HttpServletRequest params → String token params: servlet API no longer on classpath, token extraction moves to controller layer
AuthController.java (MODIFIED) - HttpServletRequest → @RequestHeader("Authorization"): servlet API removed, header extraction is now explicit - ServletUriComponentsBuilder → UriComponentsBuilder: servlet-free URI builder
── Tests ── ServiceClientReactiveTest.java (NEW) - StepVerifier tests for postReactive/getReactive JwtWebFilterTest.java (NEW) - WebFilter behavior for open/protected endpoints SecurityConfigTest.java (NEW) - SecurityWebFilterChain endpoint access rules
add missing reactor test

⏺ Step 1 (cont): Migrate integration tests from Servlet to WebFlux
LoginFlowSpringBootIT.java (MODIFIED) - MockMvc → WebTestClient: MockMvc depends on servlet DispatcherServlet which no longer exists on the Netty runtime - @AutoConfigureMockMvc → @AutoConfigureWebTestClient - All test assertions use WebTestClient fluent API (.exchange().expectStatus()...)
CustomUserDetailServiceSecurityIT.java (MODIFIED) - AuthenticationManager → ReactiveAuthenticationManager: servlet-based AuthenticationManager bean no longer exists after security migration - MockRestServiceServer + RestTemplate → @MockBean ServiceClient with Mockito stubbing: WebFlux uses WebClient internally, MockRestServiceServer only works with RestTemplate - Assertions use StepVerifier for reactive Mono<Authentication> result
RestTemplateTestConfig.java (DELETED) - Provided RestTemplate bean for CustomUserDetailServiceSecurityIT - No longer needed since test now mocks ServiceClient directly

All test package has been updated: 1) categorize by unit/component/integration/stress test.
2) for the test that need spring test initialization , EnvFile plugin configuration has been added. Maintain rule of no hard corded env value.
3) loginProcess related test need to clean up many redundant code yet.
update kafka producer connection info
Add ZIPKIN URL for config_server
update token field lenght
create delete by youtubeContentID process

1 Consumer : Mongo return DuplicateKeyException NOT DuplicateDataException
2./auth/token/refresh need to be open end point
update Jenkinsfile-cloud wrong syntax error
update gateway function for checking expiration day for both access and refresh
Remove expiration logic using ".clockSkewSeconds(Integer.MAX_VALUE) " because all Gateway will be the only place for expiration check

option.name: LAST_COMMIT_MESSAGE
option.value: Remove expiration logic using ".clockSkewSeconds(Integer.MAX_VALUE) " because all Gateway will be the only place for expiration check
